title.zh: 設計 D1 資料庫綱要（資料表與 migration）

subtitle.en: Model users and posts in SQLite, then evolve the schema safely with migrations
subtitle.zh: 用 SQLite 設計 users 與 posts 資料表，再用 migration 安全地讓綱要隨時間演進

tagline.en: A good schema is the foundation of every app — design it once, change it safely forever.
tagline.zh: 好的資料庫綱要是每個應用的地基——設計一次，之後永遠都能安全地修改它。

text.en: A schema is the blueprint of your database — the list of tables, the columns in each table, and the rules that keep the data clean. In this guide we model a tiny blog: users who write posts. You will learn primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and the NOT NULL / UNIQUE rules, then manage every future change with migrations.
text.zh: 綱要（schema）就是你資料庫的設計藍圖——有哪些資料表、每張表有哪些欄位，以及讓資料保持乾淨的規則。這篇我們會設計一個迷你部落格：使用者（users）撰寫貼文（posts）。你會學到主鍵（primary key）、外鍵（foreign key）、索引（index），以及 NOT NULL／UNIQUE 規則，最後用 migration 管理之後的每一次變更。

text.en: D1 runs on SQLite, so this is plain, standard SQL. The big idea: design the tables thoughtfully up front, then never hand-edit the live database — instead, write a small migration file for each change so your schema has a clear, versioned history.
text.zh: D1 底層是 SQLite，所以這裡用的就是樸實的標準 SQL。核心觀念是：一開始把資料表設計好，之後絕不直接手動改線上資料庫——而是為每次變更寫一個小小的 migration 檔，讓你的綱要擁有清楚、有版本紀錄的歷史。

code.en:
flowchart LR
  A["List your data"] --> B["Design tables and columns"]
  B --> C["Add keys and indexes"]
  C --> D["Write a migration file"]
  D --> E["Apply with Wrangler"]
  E --> F["Versioned D1 schema"]
code.zh:
flowchart LR
  A["列出你的資料"] --> B["設計資料表與欄位"]
  B --> C["加上鍵與索引"]
  C --> D["寫一個 migration 檔"]
  D --> E["用 Wrangler 套用"]
  E --> F["有版本紀錄的 D1 綱要"]

heading.en: The data model: users and posts

text.en: One user can write many posts, and every post belongs to exactly one user. That is a one-to-many relationship. We link them with a foreign key: posts.user_id points back to users.id.
text.zh: 一位使用者可以發表很多篇貼文，而每篇貼文剛好屬於一位使用者，這就是「一對多」（one-to-many）關聯。我們用外鍵把它們連起來：posts.user_id 指回 users.id。

code.en:
erDiagram
  USERS ||--o{ POSTS : "writes"
  USERS {
    integer id PK "auto id"
    text email UK "unique login"
    text name "display name"
    text created_at "timestamp"
  }
  POSTS {
    integer id PK "auto id"
    integer user_id FK "owner"
    text title "not null"
    text body "content"
    text status "draft or live"
    text created_at "timestamp"
  }
code.zh:
erDiagram
  USERS ||--o{ POSTS : "發表"
  USERS {
    integer id PK "自動編號"
    text email UK "唯一登入"
    text name "顯示名稱"
    text created_at "建立時間"
  }
  POSTS {
    integer id PK "自動編號"
    integer user_id FK "作者"
    text title "不可空白"
    text body "內文"
    text status "草稿或上線"
    text created_at "建立時間"
  }

text.en: id uniquely identifies each row. INTEGER PRIMARY KEY in SQLite auto-fills a new number for every insert.
text.zh: id 用來唯一辨識每一列。在 SQLite 裡 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY 會在每次新增時自動填入一個新號碼。

text.en: posts.user_id stores the id of the owning user, linking the two tables and keeping data consistent.
text.zh: posts.user_id 存放擁有者使用者的 id，把兩張表連起來，並讓資料保持一致。

text.en: An index on user_id lets the database jump straight to a user's posts instead of scanning the whole table.
text.zh: 在 user_id 上建索引，能讓資料庫直接跳到某位使用者的貼文，而不必把整張表掃過一遍。

text.en: email and name are required — the database rejects any row that leaves them empty.
text.zh: email 與 name 是必填——只要這些欄位留空，資料庫就會拒絕那一列。

text.en: email is UNIQUE, so two users can never register with the same address.
text.zh: email 設為 UNIQUE，所以兩位使用者絕不可能用同一個信箱註冊。

text.en: The crow's-foot mark (o{) means one user relates to many posts — the most common app relationship.
text.zh: 魚尾符號（o{）代表一位使用者對應很多貼文——這是應用程式裡最常見的關聯。

text.en: These are the building blocks you will use in almost every table you ever design.
text.zh: 以下是你之後設計幾乎每一張資料表都會用到的基本元件。

text.en: The full definition of your tables, columns, types, and rules — your database's blueprint.
text.zh: 你所有資料表、欄位、型別與規則的完整定義——也就是資料庫的藍圖。

text.en: SQLite uses TEXT, INTEGER, REAL, BLOB, and NULL. Store timestamps as TEXT (ISO strings) for simplicity.
text.zh: SQLite 用 TEXT、INTEGER、REAL、BLOB、NULL 五種。時間戳記建議用 TEXT（ISO 字串）儲存，最單純。

text.en: The column that uniquely names each row. INTEGER PRIMARY KEY also auto-increments for you.
text.zh: 用來唯一命名每一列的欄位。INTEGER PRIMARY KEY 還會幫你自動遞增編號。

text.en: A column pointing at another table's primary key, expressing relationships between rows.
text.zh: 一個指向另一張表主鍵的欄位，用來表達列與列之間的關聯。

text.en: A lookup shortcut that speeds up filtering and joins on a column — at the cost of slightly slower writes.
text.zh: 一種查找捷徑，能加速某欄位的篩選與 join——代價是寫入會稍微慢一點點。

text.en: Rules like NOT NULL, UNIQUE, and DEFAULT that the database enforces so bad data never gets in.
text.zh: 像 NOT NULL、UNIQUE、DEFAULT 這類由資料庫強制執行的規則，讓壞資料根本進不來。

text.en: A value auto-filled when you omit a column, e.g. status defaults to 'draft' and created_at to now.
text.zh: 沒給欄位值時自動帶入的值，例如 status 預設為 'draft'、created_at 預設為現在時間。

text.en: A numbered SQL file describing one schema change. Run in order, they give your database a version history.
text.zh: 一個有編號的 SQL 檔，描述一次綱要變更。依序執行後，就替你的資料庫建立了版本歷史。

text.en: Apps grow: you will need a new column, a new table, or a new index. A migration is a small, numbered SQL file (0001_..., 0002_...) that records exactly one change. Wrangler keeps track of which files have already run, so applying them is repeatable on every environment.
text.zh: 應用程式會長大：你總會需要新增欄位、新表，或新索引。Migration 就是一個小小、有編號的 SQL 檔（0001_...、0002_...），剛好記錄一次變更。Wrangler 會記住哪些檔案已經跑過，所以在每個環境套用都能重現一樣的結果。

text.en: The golden rule: once a migration has been applied to a shared or production database, never edit that file. To fix or change something, write a new migration. This keeps the history honest and lets teammates catch up by simply running 'migrations apply'.
text.zh: 黃金守則：一旦某個 migration 已經套用到共用或正式資料庫，就絕對不要再去改那個檔案。要修正或變更，就寫一個新的 migration。這能讓歷史保持誠實，隊友只要跑一次 migrations apply 就能跟上進度。

code.en:
flowchart LR
  A["Decide the change"] --> B["wrangler d1 migrations create"]
  B --> C["Write CREATE/ALTER SQL"]
  C --> D["apply --local to test"]
  D --> E{"Works?"}
  E -->|"yes"| F["apply --remote"]
  E -->|"no"| C
  F --> G["Versioned schema in D1"]
code.zh:
flowchart LR
  A["決定要改什麼"] --> B["wrangler d1 migrations create"]
  B --> C["寫 CREATE/ALTER SQL"]
  C --> D["apply --local 測試"]
  D --> E{"成功？"}
  E -->|"是"| F["apply --remote"]
  E -->|"否"| C
  F --> G["D1 內有版本的綱要"]

title.zh: 為什麼要用有版本的檔案？

text.en: Because the migrations folder lives in Git alongside your code. Anyone can clone the repo and rebuild the exact same schema by running the migrations in order — no manual SQL, no guesswork.
text.zh: 因為 migrations 資料夾會和你的程式碼一起放進 Git。任何人 clone 下來後，只要依序跑這些 migration，就能重建出一模一樣的綱要——不必手動敲 SQL，也不用猜。

text.en: Now wire it all together: create the database, bind it to a Worker, define the schema in a migration, apply it, add a second migration to evolve the schema, and finally query across both tables with a JOIN.
text.zh: 現在把全部串起來：建立資料庫、綁定到 Worker、在 migration 裡定義綱要、套用它、再加第二個 migration 讓綱要演進，最後用 JOIN 跨兩張表查詢。

text.en: Wrangler prints a database_id — copy it for the next step.
text.zh: Wrangler 會印出一組 database_id——複製起來，下一步會用到。

text.en: Add this to wrangler.jsonc so your Worker reaches the database as env.DB.
text.zh: 把這段加進 wrangler.jsonc，讓 Worker 能用 env.DB 存取這個資料庫。

text.en: This makes a migrations/ folder and an empty 0001_create_users_and_posts.sql file.
text.zh: 這會建立一個 migrations/ 資料夾，以及一個空的 0001_create_users_and_posts.sql 檔。

code.body: npx wrangler d1 migrations create blog-db create_users_and_posts

text.en: Open the generated file and define both tables with their keys, constraints, and indexes.
text.zh: 打開剛產生的檔案，把兩張表連同它們的鍵、約束與索引都定義好。

title.zh: 先套用到本機，再套用到遠端

text.en: Test against the local copy first; once it works, apply to the real cloud database.
text.zh: 先對本機副本測試；確認沒問題後，再套用到雲端上真正的資料庫。

code.body:
# Build the schema on the local test DB
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply blog-db --local

# See what is still pending on production
npx wrangler d1 migrations list blog-db --remote

# Apply to the real cloud database
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply blog-db --remote

text.en: Need a new column? Create a second migration instead of editing the first — then apply it the same way.
text.zh: 需要新欄位？建立第二個 migration，而不是去改第一個——接著用同樣方式套用。

code.body:
npx wrangler d1 migrations create blog-db add_published_at_to_posts

# In migrations/0002_add_published_at_to_posts.sql:
#   ALTER TABLE posts ADD COLUMN published_at TEXT;

npx wrangler d1 migrations apply blog-db --local
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply blog-db --remote

title.en: Query across tables from a Worker

text.en: prepare() builds a safe query, bind() fills the ? placeholder (blocking SQL injection), and the JOIN pulls each post together with its author.
text.zh: prepare() 建立安全查詢，bind() 填入 ? 佔位符（擋掉 SQL 注入），JOIN 則把每篇貼文和它的作者一起拉出來。

text.en: Publish the Worker — your schema-backed API is now live.
text.zh: 發布這個 Worker——你那個有綱要撐腰的 API 就上線了。

text.en: Seed some data to test
text.zh: 塞一點資料來測試

blocks.body:
npx wrangler d1 execute blog-db --remote --command "INSERT INTO users (email, name) VALUES ('mei@example.com', 'Mei');"

npx wrangler d1 execute blog-db --remote --command "INSERT INTO posts (user_id, title, status) VALUES (1, 'Hello D1', 'live');"

title.zh: 絕不要改已套用的 migration

text.en: Once 0001 has run on a shared database, editing it will not re-run, and teammates' databases will drift out of sync. Always add a new numbered migration for the change.
text.zh: 一旦 0001 已經在共用資料庫跑過，再去改它也不會重跑，隊友的資料庫就會慢慢和你的不同步。要改變更，永遠加一個新的有編號 migration。

en: Turn on foreign keys with PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON; SQLite ignores FK rules otherwise.
Add an index on every foreign key and any column you frequently filter or sort by.
SQLite's ALTER TABLE can add columns and rename, but cannot drop a column easily — plan columns thoughtfully.
Prefer NOT NULL with a DEFAULT so existing rows stay valid when you add a column.
Test every migration with --local before --remote; the local copy is fast and disposable.
Keep the migrations/ folder in Git so the whole schema history travels with your code.
zh: 用 PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON; 開啟外鍵約束，否則 SQLite 會忽略 FK 規則。
為每個外鍵、以及任何常用來篩選或排序的欄位都建立索引。
SQLite 的 ALTER TABLE 能新增欄位、改名，但不容易刪欄位——欄位請事先想清楚再設計。
新增欄位時優先用 NOT NULL 搭配 DEFAULT，這樣既有的列才不會變不合法。
每個 migration 都先用 --local 測過再 --remote；本機副本又快又可隨時丟掉重建。
把 migrations/ 資料夾放進 Git，整段綱要歷史就會跟著你的程式碼一起走。

text.en: D1 bills by rows read and written. A good index lets a query touch only the rows it needs instead of scanning the whole table — faster responses and a smaller bill.
text.zh: D1 是依讀取與寫入的列數計費。好的索引能讓查詢只碰到真正需要的列，而不是把整張表掃一遍——回應更快，帳單也更小。
